channel.title: Vox
channel.description: Updates for Vox — read selected text aloud and dictate, on-device.

item.description:
Fixed

  • Crash on rapid hotkey presses. Pressing the speak hotkey again while Vox was still working could crash the app. A new press now cleanly interrupts and replaces the one in progress.
  • More reliable text capture. Selected-text capture is more robust in browsers and other apps, with a smarter clipboard fallback that no longer reads stale or unrelated clipboard content when nothing is selected.

Improved

  • Faster, more accurate transcript cleanup. The Fast “Clean up transcript” tier downloads and runs an on-device punctuation model on first use, with an instant rule-based fallback so it never blocks.
  • Dictation download progress. The Parakeet dictation model now shows a real progress bar with a working Cancel button in Settings → Listening.
  • Capture diagnostics. Settings → Debug now shows exactly why a capture succeeded or failed (per-strategy results and timings).
  • AirPods audio quality. Opening Settings no longer holds the microphone open, so Bluetooth output stays at full quality.

Fixed

  • Microphone permission on macOS. Dictation can now request microphone access — the system permission prompt appears and the grant registers correctly. (Previously no prompt ever showed and access was stuck off.)
